chapter_context.summary: Highlights the power of a soft answer to turn away wrath, God's all-seeing eyes, the value of correction, a merry heart, and the fear of the Lord.

outline.text: A Soft Answer, Eyes of the LORD, and Wholesome Tongues
outline.explanation: This section focuses on A Soft Answer, Eyes of the LORD, and Wholesome Tongues (15:1-7), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Proverbs.

outline.text: Sacrifices of Wicked, Loving Correction, and Merry Heart
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Sacrifices of Wicked, Loving Correction, and Merry Heart (15:8-15), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Proverbs.

outline.text: Better a Little with Quietness, Wrathful Men, and Seasonable Words
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Better a Little with Quietness, Wrathful Men, and Seasonable Words (15:16-23), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Proverbs.

outline.text: Way of Life Above, the Proud House, and Fear of the LORD
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Way of Life Above, the Proud House, and Fear of the LORD (15:24-33), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Proverbs.

key_themes.title: Wisdom vs. Folly
key_themes.description: Proverbs outlines a practical blueprint for daily living, contrasting the actions, words, and consequences of the wise with those of the foolish.

key_themes.title: The Fear of the Lord
key_themes.description: The foundational principle of wisdom is a reverent awe and trust in God, which guides moral choices and shapes character.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the practical sayings of Chapter 15, what is one specific area of your speech, relationships, or work where you can apply divine wisdom today?
How does this chapter prompt you to choose the path of integrity and diligence over convenience or compromise?

cultural_historical.title: Ancient Near Eastern Wisdom Literature
cultural_historical.text: Proverbs shares structural and thematic similarities with other ancient wisdom writings, but stands unique in placing the Fear of Yahweh as the foundation of all true wisdom.
